_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Generate smaller, single-concern workflow steps in the design-creation skills

steps.description: Add an explicit step-granularity rule and a step-sizing checklist to skills/iw-new-feature/SKILL.md, skills/iw-new-incident/SKILL.md, and skills/iw-new-cr/SKILL.md. Add the canonical step-granularity rule to skills/iw-workflow/SKILL.md and have the other three reference it. Add a one-line pointer to the rule in the design templates' Agents-and-Execution-Order section (templates/design/**). Run iw sync-skills and iw sync-templates so .claude/skills/** and ai-dev/templates/** are byte-identical to the masters. Markdown-only change; no code, no schema. Flag cross-repo (IW-AI-DEV / InnoForge) propagation as an operator follow-up in the report.

Per-agent review of S01: AC1 — all three iw-new-* skills carry the step-granularity rule + sizing checklist, worded concretely and actionably; AC2 — skills/iw-workflow/SKILL.md states the canonical rule, the templates point to it, and the .claude/skills + ai-dev/templates copies are byte-identical to their masters; AC3 — git diff touches only Markdown under skills/, .claude/skills/, templates/design/, ai-dev/templates/; no scope violation.

Global review of S01..S02: all three ACs verified end-to-end; the step-granularity rule is consistent across the four skills (no divergent wording); the templates reference it correctly; synced copies byte-identical; git diff confined to scope.allowed_paths; no orch/dashboard/executor file touched; manifest JSON schema unchanged.
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill.
